dataset: QBlade sample projects and NACA airfoil data
case_id: Lifted Line Theory simulation with two turbine instances

pi_items.source: Task description specifies this output path
Task description specifies this output path
Task description: 'Place the first turbine at position (0, 0, 0)'
Task description: 'Place the second turbine 50 meters downstream at position (50, 0, 0)'
Derived from position coordinates: distance = sqrt((50-0)^2 + (0-0)^2 + (0-0)^2) = 50 meters
Task description: 'Number of Timesteps: 200 (20 seconds total duration)' = 200 × 0.1s
Metadata field - consistent with description claiming 20s total (20 > 15)

Physics research confirms 10-30% wake loss is realistic at this configuration: NREL reports 25% loss at 5D spacing and 12 m/s wind, and 10-20% reduction typical in offshore wind farms. 50m spacing with 12 m/s wind is physically consistent with this range.
Install script downloads QBlade_linux_v0.96.2_64bit.zip from SourceForge (confirmed via web fetch)

pi_items.verified_value: Lifting Line Theory (LLT) with Free Vortex Wake model
pi_items.source: QBlade v0.96 release notes (August 2015) include LLT module replacing BEM. Academic papers confirm QBlade uses Nonlinear Lifting Line Free Vortex Wake method.
Install script uses this URL. Verified via SourceForge files page showing this release exists (33.0 MB, 3,982 downloads, released 2016-09-06)
Verified by extracting QBlade_linux_v0.96.2_64bit.zip archive which contains: NREL_5MW.wpa (746 KB), NREL_PhaseVI.wpa (604 KB), SANDIA_SERI-8.wpa (398 KB), TUB_BeRTwpa.wpa (1,259 KB)
Workspace contains real NACA airfoil coordinate files: naca0015.dat (36 lines), naca2412.dat (36 lines), naca4412.dat (36 lines), naca6412.dat (63 lines) in standard x/c, y/c format

pi_items.verified_value: Standard NACA format: header line with airfoil name, followed by x/c and y/c coordinate pairs
pi_items.source: Inspection of naca0015.dat shows: first line 'NACA 0015', followed by coordinate data (1.0000 0.00158, 0.9500 0.01008, etc.)

pi_items.verified_value: Three lines with exact format: 'Upstream Power: [Value] kW', 'Downstream Power: [Value] kW', 'Wake Loss: [Value] %'
pi_items.source: Task description specifies: "Create a report file at '/home/ga/Documents/wake_loss_report.txt' with the exact format:"

pi_items.verified_value: Downstream turbine receives reduced wind speed due to upstream turbine wake, resulting in reduced power output
pi_items.source: Task description: 'The downstream turbine should show reduced power due to the wake effect'. Literature confirms wake effect reduces wind speed and power at 50m spacing.

privileged_info_summary: QBlade v0.96.2 for Linux includes Lifting Line Theory (LLT) with Free Vortex Wake aerodynamic modeling, downloadable from SourceForge (confirmed 33.0 MB file exists). The task simulates two turbine instances separated by 50 meters at 12 m/s wind speed with 0.1 second timesteps (200 total = 20 second duration). Real NACA airfoil coordinate data (naca0015, naca2412, naca4412, naca6412) provided in workspace. QBlade installation includes sample projects (NREL_5MW.wpa, NREL_PhaseVI.wpa, SANDIA_SERI-8.wpa, TUB_BeRTwpa.wpa). Minimum wake loss of 10% is physically realistic: research confirms 10-30% power loss at similar turbine spacing and wind conditions. Task expects report with upstream/downstream power values and calculated wake loss percentage, saved to /home/ga/Documents/wake_loss_report.txt, and project saved to /home/ga/Documents/projects/wake_study.wpa.
